vulnerabilities.description: La fonction `readFile` contient une exploitation de CWE-22 (FileForbiddenFileException).
vulnerabilities.impact: Exploiter CWE-22 peut compromettre la sécurité du système et nécessite l'examen approfondi des implémentations.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review the implementation and apply secure coding practices to remediate CWE-22 (FileForbiddenFileException).
vulnerabilities.remediationExplanation: The function `readFile` contains a vulnerability associated with Relative Path Traversal (CWE-22). The check `if(!path.startsWith(ALLOWED_PREFIX))` is insufficient because it does not resolve the canonical path. An attacker can provide a path like `/../../../../etc/passwd` which starts with the allowed prefix but resolves to a forbidden file. The fix uses `new File(path).getCanonicalPath()` to resolve the path to its absolute form and then checks if it still starts with the allowed prefix. This prevents traversal attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: La fonction `testDomain` contient un exploitable CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78)))).
vulnerabilities.impact: Exploiter CWE-78 peut compromettre la sécurité du système et nécessite une attention particulière pour implémenter les correctifs associés.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Examinez l'implémentation et appliquez secure coding practices pour remedier à CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78 (Exploitation de CWE-78)))).
vulnerabilities.remediationExplanation: The function `testDomain` contained an OS Command Injection vulnerability associated with `Runtime.exec` using concatenated input. The fix uses `ProcessBuilder` with a List of separate string arguments to avoid shell interpretation and injection vectors.

vulnerabilities.description: The function `testWebsite` contains an implementation exploitation of CWE-918 - Server-Side Request Forgery (Exploitation of CWE-918).
vulnerabilities.impact: Exploitation of CWE-918 can lead to compromised integrity, confidentiality, or availability of the system.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Review the implementation and apply secure coding practices to remediate CWE-918 - Server-Side Request Forgery (Exploitation of CWE-918).
vulnerabilities.remediationExplanation: The function `testWebsite` contains a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability associated with URL validation. The function accepts an arbitrary URL from the user without validation, allowing attackers to send requests to internal/private IP addresses or loopback addresses (e.g., 127.0.0.1, ::1). The fix involves validating the URL against a whitelist of allowed domains and blocking internal/private IP ranges.
